rule.hazard: non-compliance with applicable Union harmonisation legislation
conditions: product contains an AI system to which this Regulation and the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Section A of Annex I apply
requirements: provider shall ensure their product is fully compliant with all applicable requirements under applicable Union harmonisation legislation

rule.hazard: unmanaged safety risks from high-risk AI systems
conditions: in relation to high-risk AI systems
concern only risks reasonably mitigable through development or design or provision of adequate technical information
requirements: The org shall establish, implement, document and maintain a risk management system in relation to high-risk AI systems.

rule.hazard: outdated safety controls due to lack of lifecycle oversight
conditions: throughout the entire lifecycle of the high-risk AI system
requirements: The org shall plan and run the risk management system as a continuous iterative process throughout the entire lifecycle of the high-risk AI system.

rule.hazard: residual safety risks due to infrequent updates
requirements: The org shall conduct regular systematic review and updating of the risk management system.

rule.hazard: harm to safety from known or reasonably foreseeable system behavior
conditions: when the high-risk AI system is used in accordance with its intended purpose
concern only risks reasonably mitigable through development or design or provision of adequate technical information
requirements: The org shall identify and analyze the known and the reasonably foreseeable risks that the high-risk AI system can pose to safety when the system is used in accordance with its intended purpose.

rule.hazard: safety harm emerging from misuse or unforeseen use conditions
conditions: when the high-risk AI system is used in accordance with its intended purpose
under conditions of reasonably foreseeable misuse
concern only risks reasonably mitigable through development or design or provision of adequate technical information
requirements: The org shall estimate and evaluate the risks that may emerge when the high-risk AI system is used in accordance with its intended purpose and under conditions of reasonably foreseeable misuse.

rule.hazard: unmitigated safety risks identified during risk analysis
conditions: to address the risks identified pursuant to point (a)
concern only risks reasonably mitigable through development or design or provision of adequate technical information
requirements: The org shall adopt appropriate and targeted risk management measures designed to address the risks identified pursuant to point (a).

rule.hazard: unacceptable residual risk
requirements: Developer shall ensure that risk management measures result in the relevant residual risk associated with each hazard, as well as the overall residual risk of the high-risk AI system, being judged to be acceptable.

rule.hazard: unmitigated identified risks
requirements: Developer shall eliminate or reduce risks identified and evaluated pursuant to paragraph 2 through adequate design and development of the high-risk AI system.

rule.hazard: unmitigated non-eliminable risks
conditions: for risks that cannot be eliminated
requirements: Developer shall implement adequate mitigation and control measures addressing risks that cannot be eliminated.

rule.hazard: unaddressed interactions between requirements causing increased residual risk
requirements: Developer shall assess the effects and possible interactions resulting from the combined application of the requirements set out in this Section and apply those assessments to minimise residual risks while achieving an appropriate balance in implementing the measures.

rule.hazard: deployers lacking required information
requirements: Developer shall provide the information required pursuant to Article 13 to deployers.

rule.hazard: insufficient deployer training leading to unsafe operation
conditions: where appropriate
requirements: Developer shall provide training to deployers where appropriate.

rule.hazard: insufficient risk management
requirements: developer shall test high-risk AI systems for the purpose of identifying the most appropriate and targeted risk management measures.

rule.hazard: inconsistent performance for intended purpose
requirements: developer shall ensure through testing that high-risk AI systems perform consistently for their intended purpose.

rule.hazard: non-compliance with Section requirements
requirements: developer shall ensure through testing that high-risk AI systems are in compliance with the requirements set out in this Section.

rule.hazard: deployment of inadequately tested high-risk AI
conditions: at any time throughout the development process
prior to being placed on the market or put into service
requirements: developer shall perform testing of high-risk AI systems during the development process and, in any event, prior to their being placed on the market or put into service.

rule.hazard: insufficient validation against the system's intended purpose
requirements: developer shall carry out testing against prior defined metrics and probabilistic thresholds that are appropriate to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system.

rule.hazard: adverse impact on persons under the age of 18
conditions: when implementing the risk management system as provided for in paragraphs 1 to 7
requirements: provider shall give consideration to whether, in view of its intended purpose, the high-risk AI system is likely to have an adverse impact on persons under the age of 18.

rule.hazard: use of low-quality training/validation/testing data
requirements: Developer shall develop high-risk AI systems which make use of techniques involving the training of AI models with data on the basis of training, validation and testing data sets that meet the quality criteria referred to in paragraphs 2 to 5 whenever such data sets are used.

rule.hazard: insufficient data governance leading to unsuitable datasets
requirements: Developer shall subject training, validation and testing data sets to data governance and management practices appropriate for the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system.

rule.hazard: inadequate consideration of design choices, collection origin or data-preparation causing poor dataset quality
requirements: Developer shall ensure data governance and management practices address relevant design choices, data collection processes and origin, relevant data-preparation processing operations (such as annotation, labelling, cleaning, updating, enrichment and aggregation), and the formulation of assumptions about what the data are supposed to measure and represent.

rule.hazard: insufficient or unsuitable datasets and data gaps preventing compliance
requirements: Developer shall assess the availability, quantity and suitability of the data sets that are needed and identify relevant data gaps or shortcomings that prevent compliance with this Regulation and specify how those gaps and shortcomings can be addressed.

rule.hazard: bias in data leading to discrimination or harm to health and safety
conditions: especially where data outputs influence inputs for future operations
requirements: Developer shall examine training, validation and testing data sets in view of possible biases that are likely to affect the health and safety of persons, have a negative impact on fundamental rights or lead to discrimination prohibited under Union law.

rule.hazard: unmitigated biases causing discriminatory or harmful outputs
conditions: when biases identified according to point (f)
requirements: Developer shall implement appropriate measures to detect, prevent and mitigate possible biases identified according to point (f).

rule.hazard: incorrect model behavior due to use of irrelevant data
requirements: developer shall ensure that training, validation and testing data sets are relevant in view of the intended purpose.

rule.hazard: model bias due to unrepresentative data
conditions: where applicable, regarding persons or groups of persons for whom the high-risk AI system is intended to be used
requirements: developer shall ensure that training, validation and testing data sets are sufficiently representative and have the appropriate statistical properties, including as regards the persons or groups of persons in relation to whom the high-risk AI system is intended to be used.

rule.hazard: model failures due to erroneous or incomplete data
requirements: developer shall ensure that training, validation and testing data sets are, to the best extent possible, free of errors and complete in view of the intended purpose.

rule.hazard: unnecessary processing of special-category personal data
conditions: for the purpose of ensuring bias detection and correction
development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models: testing data sets only
requirements: Provider shall process special categories of personal data only when it is strictly necessary to ensure bias detection and correction in relation to high-risk AI systems.

rule.hazard: insufficient safeguards for fundamental rights and freedoms
conditions: for the purpose of ensuring bias detection and correction
development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models: testing data sets only
requirements: Provider shall implement appropriate safeguards for the fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons when processing special categories of personal data for bias detection and correction.

rule.hazard: use of special-category data when alternatives exist
conditions: for the purpose of ensuring bias detection and correction
development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models: testing data sets only
requirements: Provider shall verify and document that bias detection and correction cannot be effectively fulfilled by processing other data, including synthetic or anonymised data.

rule.hazard: unauthorised re-use of special-category personal data
conditions: for the purpose of ensuring bias detection and correction
development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models: testing data sets only
requirements: Provider shall apply technical limitations on the re-use of special categories of personal data and implement state-of-the-art security and privacy-preserving measures, including pseudonymisation.

rule.hazard: unauthorised access and misuse of special-category personal data
conditions: for the purpose of ensuring bias detection and correction
development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models: testing data sets only
requirements: Provider shall implement strict controls and document access to special categories of personal data to avoid misuse.

rule.hazard: unauthorised access by non-authorised persons
conditions: for the purpose of ensuring bias detection and correction
development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models: testing data sets only
requirements: Provider shall ensure that only authorised persons have access to special categories of personal data and that those persons are subject to appropriate confidentiality obligations.

rule.hazard: unauthorised transmission or disclosure of special-category personal data
conditions: when processing special categories of personal data for bias detection and correction
development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models: testing data sets only
requirements: Provider shall not transmit, transfer or otherwise permit access to special categories of personal data by other parties.

rule.hazard: retention of special-category personal data beyond necessity
conditions: for the purpose of ensuring bias detection and correction
development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models: testing data sets only
requirements: Provider shall delete special categories of personal data once the bias has been corrected or the personal data has reached the end of its retention period, whichever comes first.

rule.hazard: lack of documented justification for processing special-category personal data
conditions: for the purpose of ensuring bias detection and correction
development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models: testing data sets only
requirements: Provider shall include in the records of processing activities the reasons why processing special categories of personal data was strictly necessary to detect and correct biases and why that objective could not be achieved by processing other data.

rule.hazard: deployment without required documentation
conditions: before placed on the market or put into service
requirements: The org shall draw up the technical documentation of a high-risk AI system before that system is placed on the market or put into service.

rule.hazard: outdated technical documentation preventing compliance assessment
conditions: kept up-to-date
requirements: The org shall keep the technical documentation up-to-date.

rule.hazard: insufficient documentation preventing authorities from assessing compliance
requirements: The org shall draw up the technical documentation so that it demonstrates that the high-risk AI system complies with the requirements set out in this Section.

rule.hazard: missing required Annex IV elements in documentation
requirements: The org shall ensure the technical documentation contains, at a minimum, the elements set out in Annex IV.

rule.hazard: non-acceptance during conformity assessment
conditions: opts to provide the information required in Annex IV in a simplified manner
requirements: The org shall use the simplified technical documentation form referred to in this paragraph when it opts to provide the information required in Annex IV in a simplified manner.

rule.hazard: missing required technical documentation
conditions: high-risk AI system related to a product covered by the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Section A of Annex I is placed on the market or put into service
requirements: deployer shall draw up a single set of technical documentation

rule.hazard: omission of mandated information from technical documentation
conditions: high-risk AI system related to a product covered by the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Section A of Annex I is placed on the market or put into service
requirements: deployer shall ensure the technical documentation contains all the information set out in paragraph 1

rule.hazard: non-compliance with applicable Union harmonisation legislation due to incomplete documentation
conditions: high-risk AI system related to a product covered by the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Section A of Annex I is placed on the market or put into service
requirements: deployer shall ensure the technical documentation contains the information required under those legal acts

rule.hazard: inability to trace system events
requirements: developer shall ensure the high-risk AI system technically allows automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system.

rule.hazard: inability to identify and monitor safety or compliance incidents
requirements: developer shall ensure logging capabilities enable recording of events relevant to identifying situations that may result in the high-risk AI system presenting a risk within the meaning of Article 79(1) or in a substantial modification.

rule.hazard: inability to identify and monitor safety or compliance incidents
requirements: developer shall ensure logging capabilities enable recording of events that facilitate the post-market monitoring referred to in Article 72.

rule.hazard: inability to identify and monitor safety or compliance incidents
requirements: developer shall ensure logging capabilities enable recording of events for monitoring the operation of high-risk AI systems referred to in Article 26(5).

rule.hazard: inability to reconstruct usage timeline or determine the reference database used
conditions: point 1(a) of Annex III
requirements: developer shall ensure logging capabilities provide recording of the period of each use of the system (start date and time and end date and time of each use).

rule.hazard: inability to reconstruct usage timeline or determine the reference database used
conditions: point 1(a) of Annex III
requirements: developer shall ensure logging capabilities record the reference database against which input data has been checked by the system.

rule.hazard: inability to link matched input data and identify verifiers
conditions: point 1(a) of Annex III
requirements: developer shall ensure logging capabilities record the input data for which the search has led to a match.

rule.hazard: inability to link matched input data and identify verifiers
conditions: point 1(a) of Annex III
requirements: developer shall ensure logging capabilities record the identification of the natural persons involved in the verification of the results, as referred to in Article 14(5).

rule.hazard: inappropriate use due to lack of interpretability
requirements: developer shall design and develop high-risk AI systems so that their operation is sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret the system's output and use it appropriately.

rule.hazard: non-compliance with provider and deployer obligations due to insufficient transparency
requirements: provider shall ensure an appropriate type and degree of transparency is implemented to achieve compliance with the relevant obligations of the provider and deployer set out in Section 3.

rule.hazard: deployers misunderstand or misuse system due to inadequate instructions
requirements: org shall accompany high-risk AI systems with instructions for use in an appropriate digital format or otherwise that include concise, complete, correct and clear information that is relevant, accessible and comprehensible to deployers.

rule.hazard: risks to health
conditions: in the instructions for use
requirements: Include any known or foreseeable circumstance, related to the use of the high-risk AI system in accordance with its intended purpose or under conditions of reasonably foreseeable misuse, which may lead to risks to the health, in the instructions for use.

rule.hazard: conditions that degrade expected accuracy, robustness or cybersecurity
conditions: in the instructions for use
requirements: Include any known and foreseeable circumstances that may have an impact on the expected level of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity of the high-risk AI system in the instructions for use.

rule.hazard: unexpected level of accuracy, robustness or cybersecurity
conditions: in the instructions for use
requirements: Include the level of accuracy, including its metrics, robustness and cybersecurity referred to in Article 15 against which the high-risk AI system has been tested and validated and which can be expected, in the instructions for use.

rule.hazard: misuse due to misunderstanding of capabilities or intended purpose
conditions: in the instructions for use
requirements: Include the characteristics, capabilities and limitations of performance of the high-risk AI system, including its intended purpose, in the instructions for use.

rule.hazard: inability to contact provider or authorised representative
conditions: in the instructions for use
requirements: Include the identity and the contact details of the provider and, where applicable, of its authorised representative in the instructions for use.

rule.hazard: risk to safety
conditions: during the period in which they are in use
requirements: Design and develop high-risk AI systems with appropriate human-machine interface tools that enable natural persons to effectively oversee the system during the period in which they are in use.

rule.hazard: risk to fundamental rights
conditions: when the system is used in accordance with its intended purpose
under conditions of reasonably foreseeable misuse
requirements: Implement human oversight measures that aim to prevent or minimise risks to health, safety or fundamental rights when the high-risk AI system is used as intended or under reasonably foreseeable misuse.

rule.hazard: insufficient oversight measures relative to system risk, autonomy or context
requirements: The provider shall ensure oversight measures are commensurate with the risks, level of autonomy and context of use of the high-risk AI system and are implemented through one or both of the measure types described in the clause.

rule.hazard: undetected anomalies or dysfunctions due to missing built-in safeguards
conditions: when technically feasible
before it is placed on the market or put into service
requirements: The provider shall identify and build oversight measures into the high-risk AI system before it is placed on the market or put into service, when technically feasible.

rule.hazard: lack of deployer-implementable measures leading to inadequate oversight
conditions: before placing the high-risk AI system on the market or putting it into service
requirements: The provider shall identify, before placing the high-risk AI system on the market or putting it into service, measures that are appropriate to be implemented by the deployer.

rule.hazard: failure of human overseers to detect or address anomalies, dysfunctions or unexpected performance
conditions: when providing the high-risk AI system to the deployer
requirements: The provider shall provide the high-risk AI system to the deployer in such a way that natural persons assigned human oversight can properly understand the system's relevant capacities and limitations and be able to monitor its operation and detect and address anomalies, dysfunctions and unexpected performance.

rule.hazard: automation bias leading to over-reliance on system outputs
conditions: when providing the high-risk AI system to the deployer
in particular for high-risk AI systems used to provide information or recommendations
requirements: The provider shall provide the high-risk AI system to the deployer in such a way that natural persons assigned human oversight remain aware of the possible tendency to automatically rely or over-rely on the output produced by the system (automation bias).

rule.hazard: action based on unverified identification
conditions: high-risk AI system referred to in point 1(a) of Annex III
identification not separately verified and confirmed by at least two natural persons with the necessary competence, training and authority
exceptions: systems used for law enforcement, migration, border control or asylum where Union or national law considers the requirement disproportionate
requirements: The deployer shall not take any action or decision on the basis of the identification resulting from the system unless that identification has been separately verified and confirmed by at least two natural persons with the necessary competence, training and authority.

rule.hazard: inaccurate outputs
requirements: developer shall design and develop high-risk AI systems to achieve an appropriate level of accuracy.

rule.hazard: insufficient robustness leading to errors or failures
requirements: developer shall design and develop high-risk AI systems to achieve an appropriate level of robustness.

rule.hazard: cybersecurity breach
requirements: developer shall design and develop high-risk AI systems to achieve an appropriate level of cybersecurity.

rule.hazard: performance inconsistency over lifecycle
requirements: developer shall design and develop high-risk AI systems so that they perform consistently in accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity throughout their lifecycle.

rule.hazard: undisclosed accuracy metrics
requirements: org shall declare the levels of accuracy and the relevant accuracy metrics of high-risk AI systems in the accompanying instructions of use.
evidence: accompanying instructions of use (declared accuracy levels and metrics)

rule.hazard: unauthorised manipulation of system outputs or performance
requirements: org shall ensure high-risk AI systems are resilient against attempts by unauthorised third parties to alter their use, outputs or performance by exploiting system vulnerabilities.

rule.hazard: biased feedback loops
conditions: after being placed on the market or put into service
requirements: developer shall develop high-risk AI systems that continue to learn after being placed on the market or put into service in such a way as to eliminate or reduce as far as possible the risk of possibly biased outputs influencing input for future operations (feedback loops).

rule.hazard: biased feedback loops
conditions: after being placed on the market or put into service
requirements: developer shall ensure that any feedback loops are duly addressed with appropriate mitigation measures.

rule.hazard: cybersecurity compromise
requirements: The developer shall ensure that technical solutions implemented to ensure the cybersecurity of high-risk AI systems are appropriate to the relevant circumstances and the risks.
